title: Sony’s flagship RGB LED TV is incredible
summary: Sony's Bravia 9 II emerges as the year's standout RGB LED TV, delivering exceptional color accuracy and HDR performance that brings cinematic content to life with natural landscapes and vibrant special effects. The display excels at rendering both subtle environmental details and explosive moments with impressive contrast and clarity.
content:
Sony’s flagship Bravia 9 II is the best RGB LED TV I’ve seen this year.	

The Sony Bravia 9 II is the most anticipated new TV in years. It's an amazing RGB LED TV. I watched Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves on the new Bravia with my son, who has been getting into the roleplaying game but had never seen the movie. The landscapes of Faerûn looked natural and real, while the magic cast by the Red Wizards of Thay was vibrant and colorful. Specular highlights in HDR really pop. I saw it with Xenk's glowing sword as he fought in the Underdark in Honor Among Thieves, but also in the explosions as Furiosa flees across the desert in Mad Max: Fury Road and the sun reflecting off the waves in The Meg.
The Bravia 7 II …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 索尼旗舰RGB LED电视令人难以置信
zh.summary: 索尼Bravia 9 II脱颖而出，成为今年最杰出的RGB LED电视，提供卓越的色彩精准度和HDR性能，将电影级内容栩栩如生地呈现出自然风景和充满活力的特效。该显示器在渲染细微的环境细节和爆炸性时刻时表现出色，对比度和清晰度令人印象深刻。

title: More games should be on rails (literally)
summary: Nintendo's Star Fox remake and indie title Denshattack! demonstrate the enduring appeal of on-rails game design, proving that carefully scripted, action-packed sequences remain engaging years after release. The trend suggests developers should reconsider this focused approach for creating high-impact gaming experiences.
content:
It's been a good few weeks for games on rails. Nintendo's Star Fox remake wisely kept the tightly scripted, action-packed levels from Star Fox 64 largely the same, and they're still fun to fly through nearly 20 years later. Denshattack!, a new game from Undercoders, similarly features levels packed with carefully orchestrated sequences to great effect. Except instead of flying through space as an unnervingly realistic anthropomorphic fox, you're flying - and flipping, and spinning, and grinding - through Japan while driving a blindingly fast train.
Calling Denshattack! a train game radically undersells what you actually do. Across a bright  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 更多游戏应该在导轨上（字面上）
zh.summary: 任天堂的《星际火狐》重制版和独立游戏《Denshattack!》展示了导轨游戏设计的持久魅力，证明了精心编写的、快节奏的序列在发布多年后仍然引人入胜。这一趋势表明开发者应该重新考虑这种专注的方法来创造高影响力的游戏体验。

title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
summary: The Verge's Installer No. 136 newsletter provides curated recommendations for essential apps, gadgets, and tools readers should explore. The edition covers diverse topics and discoveries for anyone seeking to optimize their digital toolkit and workflow.
content:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 136, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been recording the next season of Version History (this season's finale is out on Sunday!), reading about data center heists and Backyard Baseball and the creator of Calvin and Hobbes, canceling my October plans to see Digger 30 or 40 times, taking on the new Knockout Tour routes in Mario Kart World, learning more than I ever intended about Staten Island thanks to Revisionist His …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 每个读者需要的应用、小工具和工具
zh.summary: The Verge的Installer第136期通讯为读者提供了精选的必备应用、小工具和工具推荐。该版本涵盖了多样化的主题和发现，适合任何寻求优化其数字工具包和工作流程的人。

title: Will AI fix prior authorization—or make it worse?
summary: The government is piloting AI systems to automate insurance prior authorization decisions, a traditionally slow process that delays patient coverage. The initiative raises critical questions about whether AI will streamline healthcare access or introduce new barriers for patients seeking approved treatments.
content: The government is piloting a program that uses AI for insurance-coverage decisions.

zh.title: 人工智能能改进医保预批准流程，还是会让它更糟？
zh.summary: 政府正在试验人工智能系统来自动化保险预批准决策，这个传统上缓慢的流程会延迟患者的保险覆盖。该倡议提出了关键问题，即人工智能是否会简化医疗保健获取，还是会为寻求批准治疗的患者引入新障碍。

title: Fine, electric mountain bikes don’t suck
summary: The author recounts their skepticism toward electric mountain bikes and how testing the Amflow PX Carbon Pro changed their perspective. The e-bike's powerful motor compensates for technique issues and makes steep climbs more enjoyable, challenging the purist bias against assisted biking.
content:
You don’t need a $10,000 Amflow PX Carbon Pro, but it doesn’t hurt. | Photo by Thomas Ricker / The Verge	

Cheater, I'd grumble between huffs as yet another e-bike rider casually skittered past me on a steep ascent. It's this purist attitude that, for years, has left me blind to one simple fact: electric mountain bikes are fun!
My attitude adjustment came a few weeks ago, the very first time I rode an Amflow PX Carbon Pro fitted with the incredibly compact, lightweight, and powerful M2S motor from Avinox, a new DJI offshoot that has incumbents like Bosch and Specialized on edge. The motor doesn't make me a speed demon, but it does let me suck better by compensating whenever my poor technique kills the momentum.
Mountain biking has a long histor …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 好吧，电动山地车并不差劲
zh.summary: 作者讲述了他对电动山地车的怀疑态度，以及测试Amflow PX Carbon Pro如何改变了他的看法。这款电动自行车强大的电机补偿了技术缺陷，使陡峭的上坡更加愉快，挑战了对辅助骑行的纯粹主义偏见。

title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
summary: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts that the substantial wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will eventually be redistributed, either voluntarily or by force. His prediction reflects growing concerns about AI wealth concentration in the tech industry.
content: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

zh.title: Neil Rimer认为AI产生的资金会流出
zh.summary: Index Ventures联合创始人Neil Rimer预测，硅谷AI产生的巨大财富最终将被重新分配，要么自愿要么被迫。他的预测反映了人们对科技行业AI财富集中的日益增长的担忧。
